# Project Memory

Use this file for `project/memory/README.md`.
It is the entry page for longer-lived project memory inside the human-facing control surface.
Keep it short, dense, and easy to scan.
Prefer a high-signal summary plus links to the few memory pages that matter.

## Memory Snapshot
List the few reminders a returning reader should know immediately.
Prefer 3-6 bullets.

## Read order
Which memory pages should a returning reader open first?

## What good memory looks like
What kinds of questions should this memory layer answer quickly?

## Update discipline
When should memory be updated?
Default answer: during closeout, with mid-slice updates allowed only for stable repeated memory that will affect later work before closeout.
State the de-dup rule explicitly: update existing entries in place before adding new ones.

## Store here
Which kinds of long-lived project memory belong here?

## Do not store here
Which kinds of notes should stay out of project memory?

## Split rule
When should a new memory page be created instead of growing the index page?

## Routing guide
How does this memory layer differ from:
- `project/CURRENT.md`
- `project/decisions/*`
- `ai/doc/facts/*`
- `ai/skill/*`
